generated_oml:
instance BuildingSensingComponent : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensors are defined within the enriched BIM environment, creating a unified semantic model of measurement points and continuously streaming real-time readings to the twin.",
    DTDFVocab:producedData SensorDataStream
]

instance SensorDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom BuildingSensingComponent
]

instance LiveSensorData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Continuous real-time sensor readings streamed from the physical building to the digital twin.",
    DTDFVocab:fromData SensorDataStream,
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BIMEnrichmentEnabler, ModelTransformationEnabler
]

instance BuildingActuatingComponent : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Actuators are modeled as BIM components with control logic authored by the building control engineer, enabling remote actuation of building services."
]

instance EnergyConsumptionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight into building energy consumption derived from real-time data and simulation for life-cycle optimisation."
]

instance MaintenanceSchedulingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight for predictive maintenance scheduling based on sensor trends and analytics."
]

instance AlertAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic generation of alerts to facility managers when anomalous conditions are detected.",
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ActuationCommandAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remote command invoked by facility managers to control building actuators via the digital twin.",
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Delivers real-time monitoring of building parameters via continuous sensor data.",
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyConsumptionInsight, AlertAction
]

instance HighFidelitySimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides high-fidelity simulation of operational scenarios for predictive analysis.",
    DTDFVocab:provides MaintenanceSchedulingInsight
]

instance LifeCycleOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Optimises life-cycle aspects such as energy consumption and maintenance through analytics.",
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyConsumptionInsight, MaintenanceSchedulingInsight
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Executes predictive analytics on streamed sensor data to forecast anomalies.",
    DTDFVocab:provides AlertAction
]

instance ClosedLoopControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Enables closed-loop control actions by sending commands to actuators based on analytics.",
    DTDFVocab:provides ActuationCommandAction
]

instance Interactive3DVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides rich, interactive 3 visualisations for stakeholders via immersive interfaces.",
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyConsumptionInsight
]

instance BIMEnrichmentEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Enriches BIM models with device definitions and semantic information for sensors and actuators.",
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, Interactive3DVisualizationService
]

instance ModelTransformationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Applies model-driven engineering transformations to generate digital twin artifacts from enriched BIM.",
    DTDFVocab:enables HighFidelitySimulationService, LifeCycleOptimizationService, PredictiveAnalyticsService
]

instance DataIngestionHub : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Central hub that continuously synchronises sensor readings and equipment status from the physical building to the digital twin.",
    DTDFVocab:enables ClosedLoopControlService, RealTimeMonitoringService
]

instance EnrichedBIMModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "BIM model enriched with geometric representations of sensors, actuators, and embedded control logic.",
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BIMEnrichmentEnabler, ModelTransformationEnabler
]

instance OperationalSimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "High-fidelity operational simulation model generated from enriched BIM for scenario analysis.",
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelTransformationEnabler
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Facility Managers interact with the deployed Digital Twin for real‑time monitoring and can send control commands back to the building’s control systems (e.g., lighting control). The DT Framework’s central hub enables this bidirectional flow, allowing the Digital Twin to issue control messages to physical devices. The document does not detail specific command types, message formats, validation/authentication methods, conflict resolution strategies, or confirmation of command execution."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin operates with continuous, real‑time synchronization between the physical asset and its digital counterpart, supporting real‑time monitoring and data exchange. Synchronization is performed continuously as data become available, ensuring the virtual model reflects the current state of the asset without defined batch intervals."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The system follows a central‑hub orchestration pattern: Eclipse Hono collects and normalises device data, forwards them to Eclipse Ditto which manages the twin’s state and exposes real‑time interfaces, and an Unreal Engine client subscribes to Ditto events for 3D visualization. The hub coordinates resource management and mediates between the device layer and application layer, providing integrated runtime data flow and model updates."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The framework supports a full DT life‑cycle that begins with device modeling directly in the BIM environment (enriching BIM with sensors, actuators and controller definitions). MDE (Model‑Driven Engineering) techniques then extract a structured intermediate representation from the enriched BIM model and automatically generate deployable DT artifacts. Subsequent phases include DT deployment, runtime operation with continuous real‑time data exchange, interactive visualization for monitoring/control, and later extension through modular custom‑service plug‑ins. Representation types evolve from BIM geometry/semantic models, to intermediate model artifacts, to executable DT services. Transition between phases is managed by automated model transformations and standardized extension points, although explicit governance processes are not described in the document."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Model fidelity is assessed using the Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) metric, which quantifies the degree of alignment (entanglement) between the physical building and its digital counterpart. The metric is applied during deployment to validate that the generated DT artifacts correctly reflect the underlying BIM‑derived device models and that real‑time data streams maintain consistency. Uncertainty sources include mismatches between static BIM geometry and dynamic building behaviour; these are monitored through continuous synchronization and can be corrected by updating the BIM device definitions or the generated runtime interfaces."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The solution employs a modular, service‑oriented architecture with standardized extension points and a central hub that mediates bidirectional data exchange between physical assets and the Digital Twin. While the architecture supports interoperability across heterogeneous systems, the document does not enumerate concrete network topologies, communication protocols, bandwidth or latency requirements, reliability/redundancy mechanisms, or security protocols for the underlying network communication."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "Deployment follows a model‑driven approach that automates the provisioning of digital twins through a deployment pipeline. The framework supports runtime configuration management, allowing updates without full redeployment, and is designed to scale from single components to full‑building infrastructures. While the hosting environment (cloud, edge, or on‑premise) is not explicitly named, the architecture’s modularity and low‑overhead update strategy aim to ensure high availability and reliability across diverse deployment scenarios."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Integration with external systems is achieved through Eclipse Hono’s protocol adapters (e.g., MQTT, HTTP) for device connectivity and Eclipse Ditto’s REST and WebSocket endpoints for twin interaction. The Unreal Engine front‑end consumes Ditto’s real‑time feeds, while other enterprise or IoT platforms can connect via the same standardized Hono/Ditto interfaces, forming a plug‑in style integration layer."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The proposal adopts a modular architecture with 'standardized extension points' that are intended to ensure interoperability and compatibility with existing AECO‑specific systems and standards such as BIM. These extension points define consistent interfaces for adding custom services and analytics, facilitating integration with other industry tools. No further certification requirements, specific industry standards (e.g., ISO 19650, IFC), or compliance frameworks are explicitly mentioned."
]
